grammar.title: ‘Quiero que + subjunctive’
grammar.detail: ‘Quiero que sepan…’ — verbs of wanting trigger subjunctive in the embedded clause. ‘sepan’ = 3rd plural present subjunctive of saber.

grammar.title: Three coordinated genitives
grammar.detail: ‘por el bien de ustedes y de los que están en Laodicea, y de tantos que no me conocen’ — three parallel ‘de’-phrases. Spanish lets them pile up without losing clarity.

expression.phrase: qué gran lucha sostengo por el bien de ustedes
expression.note: Paul reveals the emotional cost of his ministry — even for churches he has never visited. ‘lucha’ likely refers to prayer and intercession, not physical fighting.

grammar.title: ‘para que’ + subjunctive (purpose)
grammar.detail: ‘para que cobren ánimo, permanezcan unidos… y tengan…’ — purpose clause taking three coordinated subjunctive verbs.

grammar.title: ‘proveniente de / que proviene de’
grammar.detail: ‘which comes from’ — provenir + de is the fixed pattern for indicating origin/source.

expression.phrase: el misterio de Dios, es decir, a Cristo
expression.note: Paul defines the mystery in one breath — it is a person, Jesus, not a doctrine. The casual ‘es decir’ (‘that is’) is theologically explosive.

grammar.title: ‘en quien’
grammar.detail: Relative pronoun for people after a preposition: ‘in whom’. Compare ‘en el cual / en la cual’ (more formal) and ‘donde’ (more casual).

grammar.title: estar + past participle (state passive)
grammar.detail: ‘están escondidos’ = ‘they are (currently) hidden’. Use of estar focuses on the resulting state. Compare ‘son escondidos’ (rare/awkward) and ‘fueron escondidos’ (a past event).

expression.phrase: todos los tesoros de la sabiduría y del conocimiento
expression.note: A polemic against the false teaching at Colossae — claiming secret wisdom outside Christ. Paul replies: the treasure-house is Jesus.
